items.title: USDA seeks comments on SNAP state options for standard utility allowances (SUAs) and self-employment income methodology reporting
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a notice renewing the information collection tied to SNAP state agency reporting for standard utility allowances (SUAs) and self-employment income calculation options. Written comments are due by July 27, 2026, and the notice references the requirement that states submit SUA methodologies to FNS for approval at least every five years.

items.title: USDA renews SNAP waiver request process for nutrition incentive programs at authorized retailers; comments due July 27
items.summary: USDA renewed the paperwork collection for SNAP-authorized retailers (and partner organizations) to request waivers to offer incentives that encourage purchase of healthier foods. USDA estimates about 15 incentive waiver requests per year covering roughly 800 retailers (out of about 267,000 authorized SNAP retailers), and waivers are generally granted for three years; written comments are due July 27, 2026.

items.title: HUD outlines 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding and sets multiple HAP set-aside deadlines (many due June 26, 2026)
items.summary: HUD issued guidance implementing FFY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding, listing total available CY 2026 appropriations of $38,438,557,000, including $34,557,000,000 for HAP renewal funding and $2,835,935,000 for administrative fees. HUD set several Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) set-aside request deadlines for Friday, June 26, 2026 (5pm local time), including categories for unforeseen circumstances, portability, project-based vouchers, and other needs.

items.title: HUD deadline: PHAs must submit certain HCV portability special-fee data by May 29, 2026
items.summary: HUD’s FFY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding notice includes a reporting deadline of Friday, May 29, 2026 for IMS/PIC submissions tied to Category D (Portability) special fees. The notice describes the portability special-fee formula as up to 12 months of funding equal to 15% of a PHA’s 2026 Column A administrative fee rate per eligible port-in voucher.

items.title: Education Department finalizes Workforce Pell Grant expansion for short-term workforce programs, effective July 20, 2026 (early option July 1)
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education published a fact sheet on its final rule establishing Pell-eligible “workforce programs,” generally 8 to under 15 weeks and 150–599 clock hours. The rule is effective July 20, 2026, with an option for institutions to implement early on July 1, 2026; programs must meet accountability metrics including 70% completion and 70% employment rates.

items.title: Workforce Pell rule: students may be ineligible for Pell in an award year if non-federal grants and scholarships cover full cost of attendance
items.summary: The Education Department’s Workforce Pell final rule materials state students will not qualify for Pell Grant funds in any award year when grant and scholarship aid from non-federal sources equals or exceeds the student’s cost of attendance. The Department notes this doesn’t change the overall Pell eligibility factors, but it does limit receiving Pell when other non-federal aid fully covers costs.

items.title: IRS posts updated standard deduction amounts for tax year 2026 under the One Big Beautiful Bill provisions
items.summary: An IRS summary of “One, Big, Beautiful Bill” provisions lists a 2026 standard deduction of $32,200 for married couples filing jointly, $16,100 for single filers (and married filing separately), and $24,150 for heads of household. The same page lists 2025 standard deduction amounts of $31,500 (married filing jointly), $15,750 (single/married filing separately), and $23,625 (head of household).

items.title: IRS: 2026 adoption credit limit increases to $17,670 with up to $5,120 potentially refundable
items.summary: The IRS’s “One, Big, Beautiful Bill” summary lists a maximum adoption credit of $17,670 for tax year 2026 (up from $17,280 for 2025). The IRS also notes that up to $5,120 of the adoption credit may be refundable for 2026.
